claim: Vitamin C supplementation prevents the common cold in the general population.
evidence_snippets: Meta-analyses found no significant reduction in common cold incidence in the general population with regular vitamin C supplementation.
Some studies reported a modest reduction in cold duration (approximately 8%), but not prevention.
A stronger protective effect was observed mainly in people under extreme physical stress such as marathon runners.
label: REFUTE

claim: Regular exercise has antidepressant effects comparable to medication in mild to moderate depression.
evidence_snippets: A meta-analysis of 25 RCTs showed exercise produced a large effect size (d=0.82) on depressive symptoms.
The effect of exercise was comparable to antidepressant medication in patients with mild to moderate depression.
Both aerobic and resistance training demonstrated similar antidepressant effects in clinical populations.
label: SUPPORT

claim: Caffeine doses above 6 mg/kg provide greater endurance performance benefits than lower doses.
evidence_snippets: Caffeine at 3-6 mg/kg body mass significantly improves endurance performance.
Higher doses above 6 mg/kg do not provide additional performance benefits and increase adverse effects.
Performance improvements of 2-4% are consistently observed with moderate caffeine doses in trained athletes.
label: REFUTE

claim: Mindfulness meditation produces large effect sizes for chronic pain reduction.
evidence_snippets: MBSR programs produce clinically significant reductions in chronic pain intensity.
The effect size for pain reduction is modest (d=0.33) but comparable to other behavioral interventions.
Benefits of MBSR persist at 6-month follow-up, suggesting durable changes in pain processing.
label: REFUTE

claim: Mediterranean diet reduces major cardiovascular events by approximately 30%.
evidence_snippets: The PREDIMED randomized trial showed a 30% reduction in major cardiovascular events with Mediterranean diet supplemented with olive oil.
The protective effect was observed for both fatal and non-fatal myocardial infarction and stroke.
The cardioprotective effect was independent of caloric intake and body weight changes.
label: SUPPORT

claim: Aspirin prevents colorectal cancer after only one year of use.
evidence_snippets: Regular aspirin use is associated with a significant reduction in colorectal cancer risk.
The protective effect requires long-term use of at least 5 years and doses of 75-325 mg per day.
Meta-analysis showed a 27% reduction in colorectal cancer risk with regular aspirin use.
label: REFUTE

claim: The relationship between screen time and childhood obesity has not been established.
evidence_snippets: Higher daily screen time is positively associated with increased BMI in children aged 5-11.
Each additional hour of television viewing was associated with a 0.07 kg/m2 increase in BMI.
Randomized interventions reducing screen time produced modest but significant reductions in BMI z-scores.
label: REFUTE

claim: Sugar-sweetened beverage consumption is independently associated with increased risk of type 2 diabetes.
evidence_snippets: Each additional serving per day of sugar-sweetened beverages was associated with a 26% greater risk of type 2 diabetes.
The association persists after adjustment for total energy intake, BMI, and dietary quality.
Replacing one serving per day of sugar-sweetened beverage with water was associated with a 7-10% lower risk.
label: SUPPORT
